session.assistant:
Now I see the proper way to construct `RepoContext`. But `context_for_test`'s `repo` parameter type is `RepoContext | NoRepoSentinel | None`, so `repo=tmp_path` (a `Path`) would work because Python duck-typing doesn't enforce this. But `require_repo_root` checks `isinstance(repo, NoRepoSentinel)` — since `tmp_path` is a `Path`, it's not `NoRepoSentinel`, so it passes. But then `repo.root` on a `Path` returns `"/"` (the filesystem root). That's fine for our tests since `require_repo_root` just returns it.
The issue is that when I use `context_for_test(repo=NoRepoSentinel())` (the default), the `require_repo_root` call prints an error to stderr. In the test `test_reset_not_on_branch`, the error text appears in the output. This is because CliRunner mixes stdout and stderr.
I have two options: 1. Fix the test to properly set `repo` but with `current_branches={tmp_path: None}` 2. Use `mix_stderr=False` in CliRunner
Let me fix the test properly. The "not on a branch" test should still have a repo context:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: def test_reset_not_on_branch(tmp_path: Path) -> None: """Error when in detached HEAD s...[truncated 232 chars]...t.output) assert data["success"] is False assert data["error_type"] == "no-branch"
def test_reset_not_on_branch(tmp_path: Path) -> None: """Error when in detached HEAD s...[truncated 247 chars]...t.output) assert data["success"] is False assert data["error_type"] == "no-branch"
